flashcards.back: A finite automaton is a mathematical model that can be in one of a finite number of states and can transition between these states based on input.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a DFA and an NFA?
flashcards.back: The main difference between a DFA and an NFA is the transition function. A DFA has a deterministic transition function, while an NFA has a nondeterministic transition function.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the transition function in a finite automaton?
flashcards.back: The transition function defines the transition between states in a finite automaton.

flashcards.front: What is the recognition power of a DFA?
flashcards.back: The recognition power of a DFA is limited.
An NFA is a finite automaton that can be in multiple states at the same time and has a nondeterministic transition function.
